completed_tasks.name: Split Stopki.docx into 3 per-person footer files
completed_tasks.notes: Used PizZip + node script to split by XML section boundaries. Created Stopka_jagienka.docx (Jagienka Pieprzowska), Stopka_alicja.docx (Alicja Kozłowska), Stopka_agata.docx (Agata Truszkowska).

completed_tasks.name: Create public/ directory with template + footer files
completed_tasks.notes: Copied SZABLON2.docx + 3 Stopka_*.docx into public/ so Vite can serve them.

completed_tasks.name: Implement appendFooterToZip() in App.tsx
completed_tasks.notes: Merges footer docx body as a new Word section. Remaps rIds (prefix rIdFtr_), copies images into main zip, adds hyperlink rels. Converts main doc's final sectPr into section-break paragraph so footer gets its own page.

completed_tasks.name: Add selectedFooter state + FooterSelector UI
completed_tasks.notes: Toggle buttons (Jagienka/Alicja/Agata), shown in preview header AND batch toolbar. All 3 export buttons (single, batch zip, merged offer) disabled until footer selected.

completed_tasks.name: Wire footer into all 3 export functions
completed_tasks.notes: downloadWord, downloadBatch, downloadMergedOffer all now call loadFooterBuffer(selectedFooter) before generating. generateDocxBlob and generateMergedOfferBlob accept footerBuffer param.

remaining_tasks.name: Browser test: verify footer page renders correctly in Word
remaining_tasks.notes: Run npm run dev, upload baza danych.xlsx, select a product, pick a footer, download and open in Word. Check that the footer person's photo + contact info appears on page 2.

remaining_tasks.name: Browser test: batch export and merged offer with footer
remaining_tasks.notes: Select multiple products, pick footer, export both ways. Verify each docx in the zip has the footer page.

human_actions_pending.action: Test the generated .docx files in Microsoft Word
human_actions_pending.context: The appendFooterToZip logic manipulates XML directly — needs visual verification that the footer page appears correctly with photo, text, and layout intact.

decisions.decision: Identified 3 persons from Stopki.docx: Jagienka Pieprzowska, Alicja Kozłowska, Agata Truszkowska (NOT Kasia — user said 'kasia etc' but Stopki.docx has these 3)
decisions.rationale: User confirmed the 3 pages from Stopki.docx are the source of truth.

decisions.decision: Footer merge uses direct XML section insertion (not altChunk)
decisions.rationale: The footer docx contains real Word content with images — HTML altChunk would lose formatting. Direct XML sectPr splitting preserves all formatting.

decisions.decision: selectedFooter state is global (single selection for all exports in a session)
decisions.rationale: User wants one footer choice that applies to everything — cleaner UX than per-export selection.

uncommitted_files: Stopki.docx (source, likely not needed in git)
next_action: Run `npm run dev`, upload baza danych.xlsx, select product, pick Jagienka, click Pobierz Word (.docx), open in Word and verify footer page 2 shows Jagienka's photo and contact info.
context_notes: The appendFooterToZip function is the critical piece. It finds all r:embed and r:id refs in the footer body, remaps them to rIdFtr_{original} in both the body XML and the main zip rels, copies media files. Then it converts the main doc's final <w:sectPr> into a section-break paragraph so the footer body (ending with its own sectPr) becomes the last section. Not yet browser-tested.
